description: Evals that verify AI agents can discover GoFastr framework features when dropped into a freshly scaffolded project. Tests both the onboarding-files path (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, skill) and the docs fallback path (gofastr docs --grep).
setup: Scaffold a temp project with `gofastr init evalblog --module=example.com/evalblog` in a temp directory. Rebuild the binary first with `go build -o $HOME/go/bin/gofastr ./cmd/gofastr`.

evals.name: agent-discovers-batteries-via-onboarding
evals.prompt: You are working in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr web application project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user just created this project and needs help planning a blog site with user authentication (login, register, sessions), an admin dashboard, and blog posts with CRUD. Discover what the GoFastr framework provides by reading the project's onboarding files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, agents/ directory, .claude/skills/). Do NOT write any code. Just list which GoFastr batteries to use, key import paths, and any gotchas.
evals.expected_output: Agent should discover battery/auth and battery/admin by reading AGENTS.md and the gofastr-host skill, without needing to shell out to gofastr docs.

assertions.check: response mentions battery/auth or auth.AuthManager or AuthConfig
response mentions battery/admin
agent read AGENTS.md (evidenced by referencing battery details from it)
agent read CLAUDE.md or .claude/skills/gofastr-host/SKILL.md

evals.name: agent-finds-2fa-via-docs-fallback
evals.prompt: You are working in a GoFastr web application project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs to add two-factor authentication. CLAUDE.md mentions `gofastr docs` as a way to search the embedded framework docs. Use `gofastr docs --grep 2fa` to search, then `gofastr docs auth` to read the auth topic. Tell me the 2FA plugin name, its routes, and its import path.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses gofastr docs --grep to discover the 2FA topic, reads the auth doc, and returns TwoFAPlugin with its routes.

assertions.check: agent ran gofastr docs --grep or similar search
response mentions TwoFAPlugin
response mentions /auth/2fa/ routes (enroll, verify, challenge, disable, or backup-codes)
response mentions battery/auth import path

evals.name: agent-finds-ui-styling-via-docs
evals.prompt: You are working in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user wants to customize the look of their site — change colors, fonts, and layout. Use `gofastr docs --grep theme` or `gofastr docs ui-getting-started` to find how styling works in GoFastr. Briefly explain the theming approach.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers the theme/UI docs and mentions theme tokens, StyleSheet builder, or the ui-getting-started guide.

assertions.check: agent ran gofastr docs command
response mentions theme tokens, StyleSheet, or ui-getting-started
response does NOT suggest writing raw CSS files (GoFastr uses Go-native StyleSheet builder)

evals.name: multi-battery-integration-no-reinvent
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs user registration+login, welcome emails on signup, full-text search for posts, hourly cron to clean expired sessions, and outbound webhooks when a post is published. Discover GoFastr batteries for ALL five. You must NOT suggest writing custom cron, custom email, custom search, or custom webhook code.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers battery/auth, battery/email, battery/search, battery/queue (for cron), and battery/webhook without suggesting any custom implementations.

assertions.check: response mentions battery/auth
response mentions battery/email
response mentions battery/search
response mentions battery/queue or Scheduler or cron
response mentions battery/webhook
response does NOT suggest writing custom cron/scheduler
response does NOT suggest writing custom email sender
response does NOT suggest writing custom search
response does NOT suggest writing custom webhook

evals.name: cross-cutting-audit-access-owner
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs: (1) audit log of every CRUD operation for compliance, (2) restrict /admin to 'admin' role only, (3) posts owned by the creating user so users only see their own. These are three different cross-cutting concerns in different parts of the framework.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers WithAuditLog (framework), access control (framework/access), and OwnerField (entity declarations) without suggesting custom middleware.

assertions.check: response mentions WithAuditLog or audit-log
response mentions access control, RolePolicy, or RequirePermission
response mentions OwnerField or owner-scoped entities
response does NOT suggest writing custom middleware for any of the three

evals.name: non-obvious-features-i18n-flags-image
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs: (1) English + Spanish language support, (2) per-user feature flags toggled without redeploying, (3) auto-thumbnail uploaded profile photos to 200x200. These are NOT in battery/auth or battery/admin.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers core/i18n, core/featureflag, and framework/image without suggesting custom image resize, custom feature flags, or custom i18n.

assertions.check: response mentions i18n or core/i18n
response mentions feature flags or core/featureflag
response mentions image pipeline or framework/image
response does NOT suggest using libvips, imaging, or other image libraries
response does NOT suggest building a feature flag system

evals.name: anti-reinvent-idempotency-cursor-batch
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs: (1) idempotent API so double-clicks don't create duplicates, (2) cursor-based pagination for 100k+ records, (3) bulk-create 500 records in one request. These are NOT things to build from scratch — GoFastr has built-in support for all three.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers idempotency middleware, cursor pagination, and batch endpoints without suggesting custom implementations.

assertions.check: response mentions Idempotency-Key or idempotency middleware
response mentions cursor pagination or keyset pagination
response mentions batch endpoints or _batch
response does NOT suggest writing custom deduplication
response does NOT suggest writing custom cursor logic
response does NOT suggest writing a loop that inserts one at a time

evals.name: multi-tenant-saas-migrations-health
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user is building a multi-org SaaS. They need: (1) multi-tenant data isolation (org A can't see org B's posts), (2) database schema migrations that evolve over time, (3) a health check endpoint for their load balancer.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers MultiTenant entity flag, the migration runner (AutoMigrate + SQL files), and built-in /healthz /readyz probes.

assertions.check: response mentions MultiTenant or tenant_id scoping
response mentions AutoMigrate or migrate runner
response mentions /healthz or /readyz or RegisterReadiness
response does NOT suggest writing custom WHERE tenant_id = ? filters
response does NOT suggest writing a custom migration system

evals.name: ui-components-table-form-modal-toast-sidebar
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs: (1) a sortable paginated data table for users, (2) a validated form for creating/editing blog posts, (3) a confirmation modal before deleting a post, (4) a toast notification after save, (5) a sidebar navigation with collapsible sections. GoFastr has all of these built in — do NOT suggest raw HTML or external JS libraries.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers DataTable, form module, ConfirmAction/Modal widget, ToastStack, and Sidebar component from framework/ui.

assertions.check: response mentions DataTable
response mentions Form, FormField, or form-module
response mentions ConfirmAction or Modal widget
response mentions ToastStack or toast
response mentions Sidebar component
response does NOT suggest raw HTML table
response does NOT suggest external JS library

evals.name: ui-charts-without-external-libs
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs a line chart of signups, a pie chart of user roles, and a sparkline in the header. Find the GoFastr charting components. The user should NOT need Chart.js, D3, or any external JS library.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers LineChart, PieChart, and Sparkline — all SSR SVG, no JS framework needed.

assertions.check: response mentions LineChart
response mentions PieChart
response mentions Sparkline
response mentions SSR SVG as the rendering approach
response does NOT mention Chart.js or chart.js
response does NOT mention D3 or d3.js
response does NOT suggest any external visualization library

evals.name: ui-islands-streams-no-js-frameworks
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs: (1) live-updating notification bell via server-push, (2) search-as-you-type without page reload, (3) a like button that increments without page reload. Find GoFastr's island/stream/hydration system. Do NOT suggest HTMX, Alpine.js, React, or manual fetch() calls.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers core-ui/island + core/stream SSE + data-fui-* hydration attributes. No external JS frameworks suggested.

assertions.check: response mentions island system or core-ui/island
response mentions SSE or core/stream
response mentions data-fui-* attributes or runtime.js
response does NOT suggest HTMX or htmx
response does NOT suggest Alpine.js
response does NOT suggest React, Vue, Svelte, or any JS framework

evals.name: ui-widgets-step-rails-card-composition
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs: (1) a floating chat widget overlay, (2) a multi-step onboarding wizard, (3) a table column that renders a progress bar, (4) a reusable card component with customizable header/footer/body. Find the GoFastr primitives for widgets, step rails, custom column rendering, and component composition.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers core-ui/widget + preset.FloatingPanel, StepWizard/StepRail, DataTable cell-as-render.HTML, and Card with slot-like composition.

assertions.check: response mentions widget system or core-ui/widget
response mentions StepWizard or StepRail
response mentions DataTable cells as render.HTML or custom cell rendering
response mentions Card or component composition pattern
response does NOT suggest building a custom overlay div
response does NOT suggest building custom wizard state management

evals.name: ui-theme-layout-dark-mode-responsive-no-tailwind
evals.prompt: You are in a freshly scaffolded GoFastr project at {PROJECT_DIR}. The user needs: (1) define design tokens once that flow into every component, (2) override table/form/button styles without editing framework source, (3) dark mode variant of the entire theme, (4) site-wide header+footer on every page, (5) responsive breakpoints for mobile. Do NOT suggest Tailwind, raw CSS files, or CSS-in-JS.
evals.expected_output: Agent discovers style.Theme tokens, registry.RegisterStyle for overrides, ThemeToggle + data-color-scheme for dark mode, app.Layout for header/footer, and BreakpointSet tokens for responsive.

assertions.check: response mentions style.Theme or typed tokens
response mentions RegisterStyle or Contribute for overrides
response mentions dark mode via ThemeToggle or data-color-scheme
response mentions app.Layout or WithHeader/WithFooter
response mentions BreakpointSet or responsive breakpoints
response does NOT suggest Tailwind CSS
response does NOT suggest writing raw .css files
response does NOT suggest styled-components, emotion, or CSS-in-JS
